question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async nature of responseHandler by sending a burst of concurrent block requests that each await the same slow database query, causing all responseHandler instances to be suspended simultaneously and creating a block data delivery gap exceeding 500% of average block time for all clients?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: linkNextHeaderValue()] Can an unprivileged external user cause res.locals.responseData.links?.next to contain a URL with a block.number parameter set to a very old block number, so that linkNextHeaderValue() emits a Link header directing paginating clients to re-traverse blocks from the distant past, causing clients to miss current blocks and perceive a transaction freeze lasting 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the headers.path[path] lookup by sending requests to a block endpoint path that matches a key in headers.path with a very high cache-control max-age (e.g., /api/v1/blocks/{id} with max-age=600), causing block responses to be cached for 600 seconds which may exceed 500% of the average block time (approximately 3-5 seconds for Hedera), freezing the client's view of the block state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where res.locals[responseHeadersLabel] contains a forged x-block-timestamp header with a past timestamp, causing downstream systems that rely on this header to believe the most recent block is older than it is, creating a perceived block delay exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseHandler calls next() unconditionally after res.send(), causing the middleware chain to continue executing even after the response is sent, and if a subsequent middleware modifies res.locals[responseBodyLabel] with stale block data, that data gets cached by responseCacheUpdateHandler, freezing the cached block state for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a block endpoint that causes res.locals.responseData to be a valid object but with links set to a non-object value (e.g., links: 'string'), causing the optional chaining res.locals.responseData.links?.next to return undefined and suppressing the Link header, breaking pagination for block data and causing clients to stop advancing through blocks, freezing their view of the transaction state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the APPLICATION_JSON constant comparison (contentType === APPLICATION_JSON) by causing the content-type header to be set with extra whitespace or different casing (e.g., 'Application/JSON; charset=UTF-8'), bypassing JSONStringify() and sending raw block objects that may contain circular references or non-serializable values, crashing the response pipeline and delaying block data delivery by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a block endpoint with a very large number of query parameters, causing the path resolution (res.locals[requestPathLabel] ?? req.route.path) to produce a path string that does not match any key in headers.path, falling back to headers.default with a shorter cache TTL, and then exploit this to repeatedly invalidate caches and amplify database load, delaying block responses by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the logger.info() call (logger.info(`${req.ip} ${req.method} ${req.originalUrl} in ${elapsed} ms: ${code}`)) by sending requests with an extremely long req.originalUrl (e.g., a block endpoint URL with thousands of query parameters), causing the string interpolation and logging to consume significant CPU time and delay block response processing by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sampleRate condition (Math.random() <= sampleRate) when sampleRate is 1.0 to ensure every block request is logged, and by sending a high volume of block requests, saturate the logging I/O subsystem to the point where the event loop is delayed by 500% or more of average block time, causing a temporary freeze in block data delivery?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a block endpoint where res.locals[responseDataLabel] is set to a JavaScript Proxy object that throws on property access, causing the responseData === undefined check to pass but subsequent access (e.g., res.locals.responseData.links?.next) to throw an unhandled exception, crashing the middleware and blocking block data delivery for 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the path-based header lookup (headers.path[path] ?? {}) by sending requests to block endpoints with path values that match keys in headers.path carrying immutable or no-store cache directives, causing block responses to never be cached and amplifying database load to the point where block query latency exceeds 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a block endpoint where res.locals[responseHeadersLabel] contains a cache-control: no-cache directive that overrides the path-specific max-age in mergedHeaders, causing all block responses to bypass caching and forcing every client to re-query the database, amplifying load and delaying block data delivery by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseHandler is an async function and Node.js processes I/O callbacks in the event loop, by sending a burst of block requests that each trigger a slow JSONStringify() call, starving the event loop of I/O callbacks and delaying block data delivery by 500% or more of average block time for all concurrent clients?
